# openshift_bmc_boot (Resource)

Mounts a discovery ISO via Redfish virtual media and reboots bare-metal hosts. Use iso_url from openshift_cluster.discovery_iso_url. Supports Dell iDRAC, HPE iLO, Supermicro, and any generic Redfish v1 BMC.



## Example Usage

### Mixed vendor — Dell iDRAC and HPE iLO

The ISO is mounted as virtual media via Redfish and each host is rebooted. Discovery and installation proceed without any physical media or PXE infrastructure. This approach works with LACP-bonded NICs because the virtual media mount happens before the OS network stack starts.

**License note:** Some BMC tiers do not include Redfish virtual media. Dell iDRAC Basic (the default on entry-level PowerEdge servers) does not support virtual media via Redfish — iDRAC Express or Enterprise is required. HPE iLO Standard includes virtual media on most ProLiant generations. Check your hardware's BMC license before relying on this resource.

```hcl
resource "openshift_cluster" "prod" {
  cluster_name         = "prod"
  base_dns_domain      = "example.internal"
  openshift_version    = "4.16"
  pull_secret          = var.pull_secret
  ssh_public_key       = var.ssh_public_key

  api_vip              = "192.168.10.10"
  ingress_vip          = "192.168.10.11"
  machine_network_cidr = "192.168.10.0/24"

  create_infra_env = true
  auto_install     = false
}

resource "openshift_bmc_boot" "prod" {
  iso_url = openshift_cluster.prod.discovery_iso_url

  # Dell iDRAC (requires iDRAC Express or Enterprise for virtual media)
  hosts {
    name         = "master-0"
    bmc_address  = "https://idrac-master-0.example.internal"
    bmc_username = "root"
    bmc_password = var.idrac_password
    vendor       = "dell"
  }

  hosts {
    name         = "master-1"
    bmc_address  = "https://idrac-master-1.example.internal"
    bmc_username = "root"
    bmc_password = var.idrac_password
    vendor       = "dell"
  }

  # HPE iLO
  hosts {
    name         = "master-2"
    bmc_address  = "https://ilo-master-2.example.internal"
    bmc_username = "Administrator"
    bmc_password = var.ilo_password
    vendor       = "hpe"
  }

  hosts {
    name         = "worker-0"
    bmc_address  = "https://ilo-worker-0.example.internal"
    bmc_username = "Administrator"
    bmc_password = var.ilo_password
    vendor       = "hpe"
  }
}

output "booted_hosts" {
  value = openshift_bmc_boot.prod.booted_hosts
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `hosts` (Attributes List) List of bare-metal hosts to boot from the ISO. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--hosts))
- `iso_url` (String) URL of the discovery ISO to mount, e.g. openshift_cluster.prod.discovery_iso_url.

### Read-Only

- `booted_hosts` (List of String) Names of hosts successfully booted from the ISO.

<a id="nestedatt--hosts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `hosts`

Required:

- `bmc_address` (String) Base URL of the BMC Redfish endpoint, e.g. https://10.0.0.10 or https://idrac.example.internal.
- `bmc_password` (String, Sensitive) BMC password.
- `bmc_username` (String, Sensitive) BMC username.
- `name` (String) Human-readable name for the host (used in error messages).

Optional:

- `vendor` (String) BMC vendor: auto (detect), dell, hpe, supermicro, generic. Defaults to auto.
